abstract.text: A collection of fully stylable, drag-based SwiftUI controls — including linear, radial, range, and 2D sliders.

inlineContent.text: Sliders
provides drop-in replacements and extensions for SwiftUI’s built-in
, designed to fill the gaps the standard toolkit leaves behind.
Every control is built around the same principles:
Fully stylable
— each component exposes a style protocol so you can customise the thumb, track, tick marks, and floating label independently.
Spatially adaptive
— sliders scale to fill their container and accept an
parameter so you can place them horizontally, vertically, or at any diagonal without extra layout work.
Tick marks and haptics
— add evenly-spaced, step-spaced, or arbitrary tick marks and get haptic feedback on iOS as the thumb crosses each one.
Magnetic affinity snapping
— opt in to a pull-and-resist snap behaviour that locks the thumb to nearby tick marks and requires a deliberate extra drag to break free.
Control
Description
A linear slider that can be placed at any angle and scales its track to the available space.
A linear
range
slider with two independent thumbs and a draggable active-track segment.
A circular slider whose thumb travels around a configurable arc.
A circular
range
slider with two thumbs and a draggable active-track arc.
A 2-D slider that maps horizontal and vertical drag to two independent values.
A joystick-style 2-D control that retains its position after the drag ends.
An on-screen joystick that appears wherever the user drags within a defined hit-box.
Turns any SwiftUI
into a slider whose thumb travels along the shape’s path.
A meter-style slider with two moving parts — thumb and track — and velocity-based gestures.
All controls follow the same pattern: implement a style protocol, then apply it with a modifier:

code: // Apply a custom style to every LSlider in a container

inlineContent.text: Style modifiers cascade through the view hierarchy, so one call at a container level styles all descendants of that type.
iOS 26 or later
macOS 26 or later
watchOS 26 or later
Add the package via Swift Package Manager:
File → Add Package Dependencies
, paste the repository URL, and select a minimum version.

A value type describing the current state of an
A Slider which has a fixed frame but a movable track in the background. Used for values that have a discrete nature to them but would not necessarily fit on screen.
Both the thumb and track can be dragged, if the track is dragged and thrown the velocity of the throw is added to the tracks velocity and it slows gradually to a stop.
If the thumb is currently being dragged and reachs the minimum or maximum value of its bounds, velocity is added to the track in the opposite direction of the drag.

— the on-screen joystick that appears wherever the user drags within a hit-box.
A control that constrains the drag gesture of the thumb to be contained within the radius
of a circular track.  Similar to a joystick, except the thumb stays where the drag ended.

Joystick view used to control various activities such as moving a character on the screen.
The View creates a Rectangular region to act as a hitbox for drag gestures. Once a drag
is initiated the joystick appears on screen centered at the start location of the gesture. While
dragging, the thumb of the joystick is limited be within the
of the sticks background circle.
